response: For most HMOs, a referral is required. Please ask your PCP to submit a referral to our office.
We do not typically see pediatric patients. Please contact a pediatric gastroenterologist.
If a plan is not recognized or not contracted, we may not be able to accept it. Send your insurance card so we can confirm eligibility.
We accept new patients on a referral basis. Please have your PCP send a referral, then we can schedule your consultation.
We’re not familiar with or may not accept that out-of-area HMO. If it’s tied to Blue Shield CA, we still need the exact plan details and referral—please send the insurance card for verification.
If we can’t accept your plan or schedule, we may recommend alternate GI groups (e.g., Stanford Digestive Health or John Muir/Tri-Valley GI) via your PCP.
At times the practice may be at capacity for new patients. If we can’t schedule, we’ll suggest alternate GI options and recommend PCP referral routing.
We typically do not see pediatric patients. Please contact a pediatric gastroenterologist.
Dr. Shelby has admitting privileges at San Ramon Regional Medical Center, where he takes call, covers emergency room consults, and sees inpatient consultations.
Dr. Shelby covers the emergency room at San Ramon Regional Medical Center during nights and some weekends as part of his hospital call responsibilities.
Dr. Shelby covers the emergency room at San Ramon Regional Medical Center during nights and some weekends as part of his hospital call responsibilities.
Dr. Shelby sees patients in our clinic and performs procedures at our affiliated outpatient center. Would you like the address or directions?
Outpatient procedures are performed at Executive Surgery Center, located at 1320 El Capitan Drive in Danville, California.
Office hours are Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Dr. Shelby does not see patients on weekends unless he is on hospital call.
Our office is typically closed on weekends. If this is urgent, please go to the emergency room. Otherwise, leave a message and we’ll return it on the next business day.
That depends on the patient’s age and the reason for the visit. What is the patient’s age?
Yes — Dr. Shelby is board certified.
Yes — Dr. Shelby is licensed to practice in California.

Dr. Shelby completed his Internal Medicine residency at Brown University, his Gastroenterology fellowship at the University of Texas, and advanced training in Interventional Gastroenterology at Harvard University. He has been in private practice in the East Bay since 2000 and is board-certified in Gastroenterology.

Dr. Shelby completed his Internal Medicine residency at Brown University, his Gastroenterology fellowship at the University of Texas, and advanced training in Interventional Gastroenterology at Harvard University. He has been in private practice in the East Bay since 2000 and is board-certified in Gastroenterology.
Dr. Shelby is known to be kind, compassionate, and professional, and is well respected within the local medical community.
Yes, a referral from your primary care physician is required before scheduling a consultation with Dr. Shelby.
If we don’t see the referral, your PCP office may need to resend it. Call us and we’ll provide the correct fax number.
